findings.evidence: Lines 188-191 are `theorem pointEquiv_comp ... := unitOfPoint_comp φ f`; `pointEquiv_apply` is available at lines 131-133.
findings.fix: Use the public apply lemmas explicitly, e.g. `by rw [pointEquiv_apply, pointEquiv_apply]; exact unitOfPoint_comp φ f`.
findings.issue: `pointEquiv_comp` proves the `pointEquiv` statement by directly reusing `unitOfPoint_comp`, relying on `pointEquiv` reducing definitionally to `unitOfPoint`.

findings.evidence: Lines 214-219 are `theorem pointsMulEquiv_mapValue ... := unitOfPoint_mapValue φ f`; `pointsMulEquiv_apply` is available at lines 164-166, and `DiagonalizableGroup.lean:197-203` uses `rw [pointsMulEquiv_apply, pointsMulEquiv_apply, ...]`.
findings.fix: Rewrite through `pointsMulEquiv_apply` on both sides before applying `unitOfPoint_mapValue`, matching the adjacent `DiagonalizableGroup.pointsMulEquiv_mapValue` proof style.
findings.issue: `pointsMulEquiv_mapValue` similarly closes by exact reuse of `unitOfPoint_mapValue`, relying on the current record layout of `pointsMulEquiv`.

summary: Two wrapper-level naturality proofs still rely on definitional reduction of equivalence application instead of the public projection lemmas, making them brittle under harmless refactors of the equivalence definitions.
